evals.prompt: Build me a todo list in tina4-js with an input field, an add button that's disabled when the input is empty, a list that shows all todos with delete buttons, and a count showing how many items are in the list. The list should update reactively when items are added or removed.
evals.expected_output: Should use signal for items array, computed for count, ?disabled for button, function wrapper for reactive list rendering, new array references (spread) for add/remove, @input for value tracking, .value for input binding.
expectations: Uses signal() for the items array and input value
Uses ?disabled prefix (not disabled=) for the button disable binding
Uses function wrapper ${() => items.value.map(...)} for reactive list rendering, not static ${items.value.map(...)}
Creates new array references with spread ([...items.value, newItem]) instead of push()
Uses @input event handler to track input value
Uses .value property binding on the input element
Shows reactive item count that updates when list changes

evals.prompt: I need a tina4-js login form that posts to /api/login, shows an error message if login fails, disables the submit button while loading and when the form is invalid (email must contain @ and password must be 8+ chars), and redirects to /dashboard on success using tina4's router.
evals.expected_output: Should use api.post (not fetch), handle thrown ApiResponse errors correctly, use ?disabled with computed, use navigate() for redirect, use signal for loading/error state, conditional rendering with function wrappers.
expectations: Uses api.post() from tina4js/api, not raw fetch()
Catches errors as ApiResponse objects (err.data, err.status) not Error objects
Uses ?disabled prefix for button disable binding
Uses computed() for form validity (email contains @, password length >= 8)
Uses navigate() from tina4js/router for redirect, not window.location
Uses ${() => error.value ? html`...` : null} pattern for conditional error display, not ${error.value && html`...`}
Uses signal() for loading and error state

evals.prompt: Build a real-time chat component using tina4-js WebSocket support. It should connect to /ws/chat, show connection status, display incoming messages in a list, and have an input to send messages. Use tina4's built-in WebSocket pipe to manage message state.
evals.expected_output: Should use ws.connect with proper options, socket.pipe() for state management, socket.status and socket.connected signals for UI binding, ?hidden for conditional display, @keydown for send-on-enter.
expectations: Uses ws.connect() from tina4js/ws, not new WebSocket()
Uses socket.pipe() to feed messages into a signal, not manual onmessage handlers
Binds socket.status signal directly in template for connection status display
Uses ?hidden or conditional function wrapper with socket.connected for online/offline UI
Uses signal() for the messages array
Creates new array reference in pipe reducer ([...current, msg]) not push
Uses @keydown with Enter key check for sending messages
